seoTitle: How to Switch Portrait to Landscape in Canva
metaDescription: Change a Canva design's orientation from portrait to landscape in a few steps for presentations, banners, and marketing materials.
intro: Switching a design between portrait and landscape is a small adjustment that can make a big difference depending on where the design ends up — a banner ad, a presentation slide, or a printed flyer. This guide covers the steps to change orientation in Canva. It's aimed at entrepreneurs, marketers, and students who need design flexibility across formats.

steps.heading: Review your current orientation
steps.body: Check the design's existing portrait layout before starting the switch.

steps.heading: Follow the step-by-step guide
steps.body: Use Canva's orientation or resize settings to switch the canvas to landscape.

steps.heading: Adjust elements for the new layout
steps.body: Reposition text and images so they fit naturally in the wider landscape format.

steps.heading: Apply to business use cases
steps.body: Use the landscape version for presentations, banners, or ads where a wider format works better.

faq.question: Do all elements automatically resize when I switch orientation?
faq.answer: Not always — some elements may need manual repositioning after the canvas dimensions change.

faq.question: Can I keep both portrait and landscape versions of a design?
faq.answer: Yes, duplicating the design before switching orientation lets you keep both versions.

faq.question: Why would I need landscape over portrait for marketing materials?
faq.answer: Landscape format often suits banners, infographics, and video thumbnails better since it matches wider screen and print formats.
